info.title: Tribe Cockpit
info.description: Outil de pilotage de tribe : consolidation, drill-down, saisie, organigramme, exports.

get.description: Public login-screen config: which SSO buttons (OIDC/SAML) to show.

post.description:
Local password login: verify credentials, enforce lifecycle, set session.

Security notes:
  * Wrong email, no local password, or a bad password all yield the same
    generic 401 (no user-enumeration) and count as a throttled failure.
  * A "disabled" (revoked) account is refused with 403 even with correct
    credentials.
  * A successful login clears the IP's throttle counter and is audited (the
    break-glass account gets a distinct action for traceability).
Clear the session cookie. Stateless — nothing server-side to invalidate.

get.description:
The SPA's authorization map: role permissions + persona capabilities +
access-review status + impersonation context.

Uses ``_any_status`` so even a pending/disabled user gets a coherent (locked-
down) payload rather than a 401. Capabilities gate section visibility client-
side (and are re-checked server-side on the corresponding endpoints).

post.description: Admin starts viewing the whole app as another user (full simulation).

End a simulation: reissue a plain session for the original admin.

The admin identity is recovered from the impersonator_id carried in the
current (impersonated) session token, so no elevated input is trusted.

get.description:
Start the OIDC Authorization Code + PKCE flow: redirect to the IdP.

404s when OIDC is disabled or incompletely configured, keeping the endpoint
indistinguishable from a non-existent one. Authlib stashes the state/PKCE
verifier in the server-side session for the callback to consume.

OIDC redirect target: exchange the code, provision the user, set the session.

Authlib validates state/PKCE and the ID-token signature during
``authorize_access_token``. We then extract identity (sub/email/name/groups,
the groups claim being configurable), require at least one usable identifier,
JIT-provision via ``_provision``, and redirect into the SPA with a session.

Serve this SP's SAML metadata XML (for registering the app at the IdP).

Validates the generated metadata and 500s on any error rather than publishing
a malformed descriptor.
Start the SP-initiated SAML flow: redirect to the IdP's SSO endpoint.

post.description:
Assertion Consumer Service: verify the IdP response, provision, set session.

``process_response`` validates the signed assertion (settings use strict mode);
any validation error or a non-authenticated result is a hard 401. Identity is
read from attributes with the NameID as email fallback, then JIT-provisioned.
Uses a 303 redirect so the browser switches the POST callback to a GET of "/".

get.description: GET /api/tribes — list all tribes, ordered. Any authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List Tribes Api Tribes Get

post.description:
POST /api/tribes — create a tribe (201). Admin only.

Optional ``leader_user_id`` promotes an existing user to tribe leader of the
new tribe (404 if unknown; the break-glass account cannot be made a tribe
leader). Audited.

get.description:
GET /api/tribes/org-overview — read-only org charts of ALL tribes (one per
tribe). Admin only.

Builds a per-squad status map for the current year and a per-tribe squad count,
then assembles each tribe's org tree from its ``OrgNode`` rows.

schema.title: Response Org Overview Api Tribes Org Overview Get

put.description:
PUT /api/tribes/{tribe_id} — update a tribe.

Allowed for an admin or the tribe's own leader (``can_edit_tribe``); 403
otherwise. ``display_order`` is a global ordering concern, so it is dropped
from the payload for non-admins. Audited.

delete.description:
DELETE /api/tribes/{tribe_id} — delete a tribe (204). Admin only.

Refuses with 409 while the tribe still has squads (they must be moved or
deleted first). Side effects: users of the tribe are detached (``tribe_id``
cleared) and the tribe's org-chart nodes are deleted. Audited.

get.description:
GET /api/squads — list squads visible to the caller.

A tribe-scoped user is pinned to their own tribe; an admin (no scope) may
filter by the optional ``tribe_id`` query param.

schema.title: Response List Squads Api Squads Get

post.description:
POST /api/squads — create a squad (201). Tribe leader or admin only.

A tribe leader may only create in their own tribe (the payload's ``tribe_id``
is ignored for them and forced to their tribe); an admin picks the tribe.
Audited.

get.description:
GET /api/squads/{squad_id} — full squad detail for a year.

Requires the caller to be in the squad's tribe. ``privileged`` (budget and
other sensitive fields) is computed from ``is_squad_privileged`` and controls
what the serializer exposes. ``year`` defaults to the current one.

put.description:
PUT /api/squads/{squad_id} — update a squad.

Requires tribe scope. Only an admin may move a squad to another tribe.
Structural fields (leader assignment, ordering, KPI/budget toggles) are
reserved to tribe leaders and admins; a squad leader may edit the squad but
not those fields (403 if attempted). Audited.

delete.description:
DELETE /api/squads/{squad_id} — delete a squad (204). Tribe leader or admin
only, within tribe scope.

Side effect: org-chart nodes and feed posts that pointed at this squad are
detached (their ``squad_id`` is cleared) rather than deleted. Audited.

get.description:
GET /api/squads/{squad_id}/dependents — milestones in *other* squads that
declared a dependency on this squad.

A dependency targets this squad directly, or this squad's tribe. This lets a
squad surface what other teams are waiting on from them ("le faire apparaître").
Requires tribe scope on the squad; ``year`` defaults to the current one.

schema.title: Response Squad Dependents Api Squads  Squad Id  Dependents Get

get.description:
GET /api/squads/{squad_id}/roadmap.pptx — roadmap-only PowerPoint for a
single squad (title slide + roadmap slide).

Gated by the ``squad_content``/``roadmap`` module and tribe scope. Returns 501
if python-pptx is not installed.

GET /api/squads/{squad_id}/roadmap.html — roadmap-only web page for a
single squad. Gated by the roadmap module and tribe scope.

put.description:
PUT /api/squads/{squad_id}/quarter-progress — set the squad's progress %
and comment for a given year/quarter (upsert).

Squad-leader reporting: requires ``assert_can_edit_squad``. Audited, then
emits ``notify_change(..., "progress", ...)``.

PUT /api/squads/{squad_id}/budget — set the squad's budget line for a year
(upsert).

Requires ``assert_can_edit_squad`` and the squad's budget module to be enabled
(403 otherwise). Business rule: the total envelope is a tribe-leader/admin
decision — a squad leader may only report spent/forecast/comment, so an
incoming ``total`` from a squad leader is ignored. Audited, then
``notify_change(..., "budget", ...)``.

post.description:
POST /api/squads/{squad_id}/key-messages — add a curated key message
(success/alert/risk) for a year (201).

Squad leader (or admin) only. Audited, then ``notify_change(..., "key_message",
...)``. ``year`` defaults to the current one.

put.description:
PUT /api/squads/{squad_id}/key-messages/{msg_id} — update a key message.

Squad leader (or admin) only, message must belong to the squad. Audited, then
``notify_change(..., "key_message", ...)``.

delete.description:
DELETE /api/squads/{squad_id}/key-messages/{msg_id} — delete a key message
(204).

Squad leader (or admin) only, message must belong to the squad. Audited, then
``notify_change(..., "key_message", ...)``.

get.description:
GET /api/dashboard — squad cards + summary for a year.

Requires the ``dashboard`` module and capability. Squads are scoped to the
caller's visible tribe; an admin (no scope) may filter by ``tribe_id``. Cards
are ordered worst-first (risk, then blocked count, then name), and the summary
aggregates totals (blocked/at-risk milestones, stale squads, average
progress). ``year`` defaults to the current one.

Return a tribe's org chart as a nested tree.

GET /api/org?tribe_id=...
Access: any authenticated user (viewing is unrestricted). Defaults the tribe
via _resolve_tribe; returns an empty list when no tribe exists.

schema.title: Response Get Org Api Org Get

post.description:
Create an org-chart node.

POST /api/org
Access: org editor. Admins may target any tribe (payload.tribe_id); every other
editor is forced onto their own tribe. assert_tribe_scope re-checks the result.
Business rules: a parent, when given, must belong to the same tribe.
Side effects: writes an "org.create" audit entry.

put.description:
Partially update an org-chart node.

PUT /api/org/{node_id}
Access: org editor scoped to the node's tribe (assert_tribe_scope).
Business rules: a node cannot be its own parent, and a new parent must be in
the same tribe. Side effects: writes an "org.update" audit entry.

delete.description:
Delete an org-chart node, re-parenting its children.

DELETE /api/org/{node_id} -> 204 No Content
Access: org editor scoped to the node's tribe (assert_tribe_scope).
Business rules: children are re-attached to the deleted node's parent so the
tree stays connected. Side effects: writes an "org.delete" audit entry.

get.description: Selectable top-level branches (the root's direct children) for the picker.

Render the org chart as a standalone HTML page.

GET /api/org/export.html
Access: any authenticated user (same module/capability gate as viewing).
node_ids, when given, restricts the export to the selected top-level branches.

Render the org chart as a single-slide PPTX download.

GET /api/org/export.pptx
Access: any authenticated user (same module/capability gate as viewing).
node_ids, when given, restricts the export to the selected top-level branches.

post.description:
POST /api/objectives — create an objective for a squad (201).

Requires ``assert_can_manage_objectives`` on the target squad. Audited, then
``notify_change(..., "objectives", ...)``.

put.description:
PUT /api/objectives/{objective_id} — update an objective.

Requires ``assert_can_manage_objectives`` on the objective's squad. Audited,
then ``notify_change(..., "objectives", ...)``.

delete.description:
DELETE /api/objectives/{objective_id} — delete an objective (204).

Requires ``assert_can_manage_objectives`` on the objective's squad. Audited,
then ``notify_change(..., "objectives", ...)``.

get.description:
GET /api/roadmap-items/themes — distinct existing milestone themes,
most-used first, for reuse/autocomplete.

Requires ``require_writer``. Scoped to the writer's visibility: admins see
every theme, others see the themes used across their own tribe's squads.

schema.title: Response List Themes Api Roadmap Items Themes Get

post.description:
POST /api/roadmap-items — create a milestone (201).

Writer who leads the target squad only (``assert_leads_squad``). The
dependency reference is normalized and the linked objective validated before
insert. Audited, then ``notify_change(..., "roadmap", ...)``.

put.description:
PUT /api/roadmap-items/{item_id} — update a milestone.

Writer who leads the milestone's squad only. Dependency fields are re-
normalized and the objective link re-validated only when those fields are part
of the update. Audited, then ``notify_change(..., "roadmap", ...)``.

delete.description:
DELETE /api/roadmap-items/{item_id} — delete a milestone (204).

Writer who leads the milestone's squad only. Audited, then
``notify_change(..., "roadmap", ...)``.

get.description: Roadmap matrix scoped to the caller's visibility (admin may target a tribe).

post.description:
Create a KPI on a squad.

POST /api/kpis
Access: writer role + edit rights on the target squad (assert_can_edit_squad).
Side effects: writes a "kpi.create" audit entry.
Returns 404 if the referenced squad does not exist.

put.description:
Partially update a KPI.

PUT /api/kpis/{kpi_id}
Access: writer role + edit rights on the KPI's squad.
Side effects: writes a "kpi.update" audit entry (with the changed fields).
Only fields present in the payload are applied (exclude_unset). 404 if unknown.

delete.description:
Delete a KPI.

DELETE /api/kpis/{kpi_id} -> 204 No Content
Access: writer role + edit rights on the KPI's squad.
Side effects: writes a "kpi.delete" audit entry. 404 if unknown.

post.description:
Add a member to a squad.

POST /api/members
Access: writer role + edit rights on the target squad (assert_can_edit_squad).
Side effects: writes a "member.create" audit entry.
Returns 404 if the referenced squad does not exist.

put.description:
Partially update a member.

PUT /api/members/{member_id}
Access: writer role + edit rights on the member's squad.
Side effects: writes a "member.update" audit entry (with the changed fields).
Only fields present in the payload are applied (exclude_unset). 404 if unknown.

delete.description:
Remove a member from a squad.

DELETE /api/members/{member_id} -> 204 No Content
Access: writer role + edit rights on the member's squad.
Side effects: writes a "member.delete" audit entry. 404 if unknown.

post.description:
Freeze the squad's current reporting cycle into a new snapshot.

POST /api/squads/{squad_id}/snapshots
Access: writer role + edit rights on the squad; additionally gated by the
`reporting` capability. Business rules: defaults to the current year, and
auto-labels the cycle "Soumission N" when no label is provided.
Side effects: writes a "cycle.submit" audit entry.

get.description:
List a squad's snapshots (metadata only), newest first.

GET /api/squads/{squad_id}/snapshots
Access: any user who can see the squad. 404 if the squad is unknown.

schema.title: Response List Snapshots Api Squads  Squad Id  Snapshots Get

get.description:
Return one full snapshot (frozen payload included).

GET /api/squads/{squad_id}/snapshots/{snapshot_id}
Access: any user who can see the squad. 404 if the snapshot is missing or does
not belong to this squad (the squad_id/snapshot_id pairing is verified).

Diff a snapshot against the squad's immediately preceding one.

GET /api/squads/{squad_id}/snapshots/{snapshot_id}/compare
Access: any user who can see the squad. Returns the current and previous
payloads plus a per-section diff (added/changed/removed). When there is no
earlier snapshot, `previous` is null and everything reads as "added".

schema.title: Response Compare To Previous Api Squads  Squad Id  Snapshots  Snapshot Id  Compare Get

get.description:
List feed posts visible to the caller, newest first (pinned on top).

GET /api/feed?squad_id=...&kind=...
Access: any authenticated user; results are limited to the caller's tribe scope
(visible_tribe_id) plus global posts. Optional squad_id / kind filters.
Business rule: the `feed_retention_days` general setting hides posts older than
the cutoff (pinned posts are always kept).

schema.title: Response List Feed Api Feed Get

post.description:
Publish a feed post.

POST /api/feed
Access: any authenticated user, but when the `feed_post_scope` setting is
"leaders" (default) only admin/tribe/squad leaders may post.
Business rule: the post's tribe is the author's tribe, or (for an admin tagging
a squad) that squad's tribe, else global.
Side effects: fans out new-post notifications; writes a "feed.post" audit entry.

delete.description:
Delete a feed post.

DELETE /api/feed/{post_id} -> 204 No Content
Access: the post's author, or an admin / tribe leader.
Side effects: writes a "feed.delete" audit entry.

put.description:
Pin or unpin a post (pinned posts sort to the top and bypass retention).

PUT /api/feed/{post_id}/pin
Access: writer role; additionally gated by the `feed > pin` sub-module.
Side effects: writes a "feed.pin" audit entry.

post.description:
Reply to a post.

POST /api/feed/{post_id}/replies
Access: any authenticated user; gated by the `feed > replies` sub-module.
Side effects: fans out reply notifications; writes a "feed.reply" audit entry.
Returns the whole post (with the new reply) so the client can refresh in place.

delete.description:
Delete a reply.

DELETE /api/feed/replies/{reply_id} -> 204 No Content
Access: the reply's author, or an admin / tribe leader.

post.description:
Toggle the caller's reaction of a given kind on a post.

POST /api/feed/{post_id}/reactions
Access: any authenticated user; gated by the `feed > reactions` sub-module.
Business rule: idempotent toggle - an existing reaction of the same kind is
removed, otherwise it is added. Returns the refreshed post.

get.description:
Return the caller's 40 most recent notifications plus an unread count.

GET /api/notifications
Access: any authenticated user (own inbox only); gated by `notifications > inapp`.

post.description:
Mark all of the caller's unread notifications as read.

POST /api/notifications/read-all
Access: any authenticated user (own inbox only); gated by `notifications > inapp`.

Mark a single notification as read.

POST /api/notifications/{notif_id}/read
Access: any authenticated user; gated by `notifications > inapp`.
Returns 404 when the notification does not exist or belongs to someone else
(ownership check doubles as the access guard).

get.description:
Return the caller's notification preferences.

GET /api/me/preferences
Access: any authenticated user (own settings). No module gate.

put.description:
Update the caller's notification preferences.

PUT /api/me/preferences
Access: any authenticated user (own settings). No module gate.
Side effect: toggling `subscribe_weekly_report` also (un)registers the global
dashboard report subscription so every representation stays in sync.

get.description:
GET /api/admin/users — list users.

Admins and tribe leaders only. A tribe leader sees only users of their own
tribe (scope from ``users_scope_tribe``); an admin sees everyone.

schema.title: Response List Users Api Admin Users Get

post.description:
POST /api/admin/users — create a user (201).

Admins and tribe leaders only. The actor must be allowed to assign the
requested role, and a tribe leader may only create users inside their own
tribe. Email must be unique. Writes a ``user.create`` audit entry.

put.description:
PUT /api/admin/users/{user_id} — update a user.

Admins and tribe leaders only, and the target must be inside the actor's
scope (``can_manage_user``). Enforces several rules: role changes stay within
what the actor may assign; the break-glass account must remain admin; only an
admin may move a user to a different tribe. Writes a ``user.update`` audit.

delete.description:
DELETE /api/admin/users/{user_id} — delete a user (204).

Admins and tribe leaders only, target must be in the actor's scope. The
break-glass account cannot be deleted, and nobody may delete their own
account. Writes a ``user.delete`` audit entry.

get.description: GET /api/admin/settings — read general application settings. Admin only.

put.description: PUT /api/admin/settings — update general settings. Admin only; audited.

get.description: GET /api/admin/auth-config — read the OIDC/SAML auth config. Admin only.

put.description:
PUT /api/admin/auth-config — update the OIDC/SAML auth config. Admin only;
audits which providers are enabled.

get.description: GET /api/admin/smtp-config — read outbound email (SMTP) config. Admin only.

put.description: PUT /api/admin/smtp-config — update the SMTP config. Admin only; audited.

post.description:
POST /api/admin/smtp-config/test — send a test email to check SMTP.

Admin only. Sends to ``payload.to`` or, by default, the admin's own address.
Fails with 400 if SMTP is disabled.

get.description:
GET /api/admin/personas — list personas (roles) and the full capability
catalogue the UI can toggle. Admin only.

put.description:
PUT /api/admin/personas — replace the persona definitions. Admin only.

Side effect: any user whose persona no longer exists is downgraded to
``member`` (the break-glass account is left untouched) so nobody is stranded
with an invalid role. Audited.

get.description: GET /api/admin/modules-config — read feature-module enablement. Admin only.

put.description:
PUT /api/admin/modules-config — enable/disable feature modules. Admin only;
audits which modules ended up enabled.

get.description:
GET /api/admin/report-config — read the weekly-report schedule/recipients.
Admin only.

put.description:
PUT /api/admin/report-config — update the weekly-report schedule. Admin
only; audited. ``last_sent_week`` is scheduler bookkeeping and is stripped
from the payload so the UI can never overwrite it.

post.description:
POST /api/admin/report-config/test — send the weekly report now to the
admin (or a chosen address) as a check. Admin only.

Builds the current-year report, renders the HTML body and (if python-pptx is
available) attaches the PPTX. Fails with 400 if SMTP is disabled. Audited.

get.description:
GET /api/admin/change-notify-config — read the on-modification email config.
Admin only. Also returns the full ``_all_events`` catalogue so the UI can
render every available trigger condition.

put.description:
PUT /api/admin/change-notify-config — update the on-modification email
config. Admin only; audited. Keys prefixed with ``_`` are UI-only helpers
(e.g. ``_all_events``) and are stripped before saving.

post.description:
POST /api/admin/change-notify-config/test — send a sample change
notification (a real squad's export) to verify setup. Admin only.

Uses ``payload.squad_id`` or the first squad by display order. Fails with 400
if SMTP is disabled or no squad exists. Audited.

get.description:
GET /api/admin/log-export-config — read the audit-log export config (e.g.
SIEM destination). Admin only; secrets stay masked.

put.description:
PUT /api/admin/log-export-config — update the log-export config. Admin
only; audited.

post.description:
POST /api/admin/log-export-config/test — push a single synthetic entry to
the configured destination to check connectivity. Admin only.

Reads the config with secrets revealed (needed to actually connect). Fails
with 400 if export is disabled. Audited.

POST /api/admin/log-export-config/flush — export the most recent audit
entries on demand. Admin only.

``payload.limit`` (default 200) is clamped to 1..1000. Fails with 400 if
export is disabled. Audited.

get.description:
GET /api/admin/tls-config — read the current TLS status (active cert +
trusted CAs). Admin only.

post.description:
POST /api/admin/tls-config/enabled - toggle in-app TLS termination.

Body: ``{"enabled": bool}``. When false the app serves plain HTTP and the
infrastructure (Gateway/ALB) terminates TLS. Applied at the next server start
(the listener is bound at boot), so the response's ``tls_running`` may still
differ from ``tls_enabled`` until then. Admin only. Audited.

POST /api/admin/tls-config/self-signed — (re)generate a self-signed cert.
Admin only.

``cn`` defaults to ``localhost``; ``sans`` may be a list or a comma/newline
separated string. Invalid input yields 400. Audited.

POST /api/admin/tls-config/import-pem — install a cert + private key from
PEM (uploaded files or pasted text). Admin only.

Both cert and key are required (400 otherwise); an optional ``passphrase``
decrypts the key. Parse/validation errors yield 400. Audited.

POST /api/admin/tls-config/import-pfx — install a cert + key from a PKCS#12
(.pfx) bundle. Admin only.

Empty file yields 400; a wrong password or malformed bundle also yields 400.
Audited.

POST /api/admin/tls-config/ca — add a trusted CA certificate (upload or
pasted text), with an optional friendly ``name``. Admin only.

Empty/invalid input yields 400. Audited.

delete.description:
DELETE /api/admin/tls-config/ca/{ca_id} — remove a trusted CA. Admin only.
Unknown id yields 404. Audited.

get.description:
GET /api/admin/tls-config/ca/{ca_id}/download — download a trusted CA as a
PEM attachment. Admin only. Unknown id yields 404.

GET /api/admin/tls-config/active/download — download the active server
certificate as a PEM attachment. Admin only. 404 if none is set.

GET /api/admin/runtime : read-only runtime diagnostics (version, host, uptime,
serving mode, whether a restart is pending). Admin only.

post.description:
POST /api/admin/restart : gracefully restart the application.

Schedules a SIGTERM on our own process right after this response; uvicorn drains
in-flight requests and exits, and the orchestrator (Docker/Kubernetes) restarts
the container with the current configuration applied. Admin only. Audited.
Returns whether the restart was scheduled and whether a supervisor will bring the
process back (``auto_restart``).

get.description:
GET /api/admin/logs?limit=&level= : recent application log records from the
in-memory ring buffer (oldest first), plus the current level and buffer stats.
``level`` filters to that severity and above. Admin only.

GET /api/admin/logs/download?fmt=txt|json&level= : download the buffered logs
as a text or NDJSON attachment. Admin only. Audited.

post.description:
POST /api/admin/log-level : set the live log level (root + uvicorn loggers).

Body: ``{"level": "DEBUG"|..., "persist": bool}``. When ``persist`` is true the
level is stored and re-applied on the next restart. Admin only. Audited.
POST /api/admin/logs/clear : empty the in-memory log buffer. Admin only. Audited.

get.description:
GET /api/admin/api-keys — list API keys (public view: prefix only, never
the secret) plus the catalogue of assignable scopes. Admin only.

post.description:
POST /api/admin/api-keys — mint a service API key (201). Admin only.

The plaintext secret is returned HERE AND NOWHERE ELSE (only its hash is
stored). Requires a name and at least one valid scope; an optional
``expires_in_days`` (>= 1) sets expiry, and ``tribe_id`` scopes the key to a
tribe. Invalid input yields 400. Audited.

POST /api/admin/api-keys/{key_id}/revoke — revoke a key. Admin only.

Revoking is immediate and irreversible; the row is kept (not deleted) for the
audit trail. Idempotent — re-revoking an already-revoked key is a no-op.
Unknown id yields 404. Audited on the first revoke.

delete.description:
DELETE /api/admin/api-keys/{key_id} — permanently delete a key (204). Admin
only. Unlike revoke, this removes the row entirely. Unknown id yields 404.
Audited.

get.description:
GET /api/admin/import-org/template — download the blank Excel template
(4 sheets: Tribu / Squads / Initiatives / OTD) to fill in. Admin only.

post.description:
POST /api/admin/import-org — upload a filled Excel (.xlsx) or YAML file and
import the organisation (tribe, squads + leaders, initiatives, OTD). Admin
only. Idempotent: re-running updates existing rows instead of duplicating.

Returns a summary of what was processed. 400 if the file is the wrong format
or has no tribe. Audited.

get.description:
GET /api/admin/import-steerco/template?squad_id= : download the Steerco Excel
template (Infos / KPIs / SLA / Incidents / Evenements) to fill in. Admin only.

With ``squad_id`` the workbook is built *for that squad*: its name is pre-filled
and the KPI / SLA rows are the ones it actually reports, so the file matches the
app instead of proposing a canned list. Without it, the standard structure.

post.description:
POST /api/admin/import-steerco : upload a filled Steerco Excel and write the
squad's monthly snapshots (12-month history + full current month). Admin only.
Idempotent per (squad, period). 400 on wrong format / unknown squad. Audited.

get.description:
Return the most recent audit-log entries, newest first.

GET /api/audit-log?limit=...
Access: admin only (require_admin). The limit is clamped to 1..1000 to bound
the response regardless of the value requested.

schema.title: Response List Audit Api Audit Log Get

get.description:
Weekly report as a standalone HTML page.

GET /api/reports/weekly.html
Access: human via `dashboard` capability, or API key with `reports:read` scope
(the _weekly_caller); gated by the `review > weekly_report` module. squad_id
narrows to one squad, else the caller's tribe scope (admins may target a tribe).

Weekly report as a PPTX download.

GET /api/reports/weekly.pptx
Access/scoping identical to weekly_html. Returns 501 when the python-pptx
backend is not installed.

Dashboard view as a page: the overview the user sees (summary + squads +
per-squad detail), optionally restricted to a chosen set of squads.
Dashboard view as a branded deck, optionally restricted to chosen squads.
Roadmap web page scoped to the caller (optionally restricted to chosen squads).
Roadmap deck scoped to the caller (optionally restricted to chosen squads).
Milestone dependencies as a page (grouped by the entity waited on).
Milestone-dependency deck (paginated table grouped by the entity waited on).

List the caller's report subscriptions (dashboard + any per-squad ones).

GET /api/reports/subscriptions
Access: cookie user with the `dashboard` capability; gated by `review >
weekly_report`. Personal, so no API-key surface.

schema.title: Response List My Subscriptions Api Reports Subscriptions Get

get.description:
Return the caller's subscription for a given squad (or the global dashboard
one when squad_id is omitted).

GET /api/reports/subscription?squad_id=...
Access: cookie user with `dashboard`; gated by `review > weekly_report`.

put.description:
Create or update the caller's report subscription (cadence/weekdays/hour).

PUT /api/reports/subscription
Access: cookie user with `dashboard`; gated by `review > weekly_report`.
Business rules: a per-squad subscription requires visibility of that squad
(404 otherwise); for the global (squad_id=None) subscription the legacy
User.report_* flags are kept in sync with the new schedule.

post.description:
Send the report now to a chosen address (HTML body + PPTX attachment).

POST /api/reports/weekly/email
Access: cookie user with `dashboard`; gated by `review > weekly_report`.
Requires SMTP to be enabled (400 otherwise); defaults the recipient to the
caller's own e-mail. Sends HTML-only if the PPTX backend is unavailable, and
returns 502 when the actual send fails.

get.description:
List a squad's review actions.

GET /api/squads/{squad_id}/actions
Access: anyone within the squad's tribe scope (assert_tribe_scope).
Ordering: open items first, then by due date (nulls last), then id.

schema.title: Response List Actions Api Squads  Squad Id  Actions Get

post.description:
Create a review action on a squad.

POST /api/squads/{squad_id}/actions
Access: writer role + edit rights on the squad (assert_can_edit_squad).
Side effects: records the creator and writes a "review_action.create" audit entry.

put.description:
Partially update a review action (e.g. mark it done, change owner/due date).

PUT /api/actions/{action_id}
Access: writer role + edit rights on the action's squad.
Only fields present in the payload are applied (exclude_unset). 404 if unknown.

delete.description:
Delete a review action.

DELETE /api/actions/{action_id} -> 204 No Content
Access: writer role + edit rights on the action's squad. 404 if unknown.

get.description:
GET /api/initiatives — flat list of initiatives, visible to everyone in
scope (read-only for non tribe-leaders).

Scope: an admin may target any ``tribe_id``; others are pinned to their
visible tribe. Optionally filtered to a single ``squad_id`` (for that squad's
report) and a ``year`` (defaults to current).

schema.title: Response List Initiatives Api Initiatives Get

post.description:
POST /api/initiatives — create an initiative (201). Tribe leader or admin.

Requires ``assert_can_manage_tribe_reporting`` for the target tribe (404 if
the tribe is unknown) and that any assigned squad belongs to that tribe.
Audited.

put.description:
PUT /api/initiatives/{initiative_id} — update an initiative. Tribe leader
or admin.

Requires ``assert_can_manage_tribe_reporting`` for the initiative's tribe;
any (re)assigned squad is re-validated against that tribe. Audited.

delete.description:
DELETE /api/initiatives/{initiative_id} — delete an initiative (204). Tribe
leader or admin, requires ``assert_can_manage_tribe_reporting``. Audited.

get.description:
GET /api/initiatives/report.html — flat initiatives list as a standalone
page. Any authenticated user; scoped like the list endpoint (admins may target
a tribe, others are pinned).

GET /api/initiatives/report.pptx — flat initiatives list as a branded deck.

Any authenticated user; scoped like the list endpoint. Returns 501 if
python-pptx is not installed.

GET /api/otds/candidate-jalons — milestones of the scope's squads, for
assigning them to an OTD. Tribe leader or admin.

Scope: an admin may target any ``tribe_id``; a tribe leader is pinned to their
tribe. Each row carries its current ``otd_id`` so the UI can show what is
already assigned. ``year`` defaults to current.

OTDs with their derived on-time status and member milestones.

Visibility (rule #4): managed by the tribe leader (or admin); a squad leader
sees ONLY the OTDs that include a milestone of a squad they lead; anyone else
sees none.

post.description:
POST /api/otds — create an OTD (201). Tribe leader or admin.

Requires ``assert_can_manage_tribe_reporting`` for the target tribe (404 if
unknown). Any assigned owner must be a squad leader of that tribe
(``_validate_owner``, a cross-tribe-disclosure guard). Audited.

put.description:
PUT /api/otds/{otd_id} — update an OTD. Tribe leader or admin.

Requires ``assert_can_manage_tribe_reporting`` for the OTD's tribe; a changed
owner is re-validated (``_validate_owner``). Audited.

delete.description:
DELETE /api/otds/{otd_id} — delete an OTD (204). Tribe leader or admin,
requires ``assert_can_manage_tribe_reporting``.

Side effect: member milestones keep existing; only their ``otd_id`` link is
cleared. Audited.

put.description:
PUT /api/otds/{otd_id}/jalons — set the milestones that make up this OTD
(replaces the current set). Tribe leader or admin.

Requires ``assert_can_manage_tribe_reporting``. Only milestones from the OTD's
tribe are accepted (400 otherwise); this is the ONLY place the milestone<->OTD
link is managed (squad leaders never touch it). Audited.

get.description:
Pending requests + the options THIS reviewer may assign (roles, squads,
tribes), so the SPA can render a correctly-scoped validation form.

post.description:
Validate a pending account with the requested role/tribe/squad.

Delegation limits (which roles/tribes/squads this reviewer may grant) are
enforced inside ``acc.approve`` and surface as 4xx errors.

Reject/revoke an account (disables it). Restricted to admin & tribe leaders
inside ``acc.deny``; the break-glass account cannot be denied.

get.description:
List leave types (active only by default; include_inactive shows retired ones).

GET /api/leaves/types
Access: any authenticated user (types are visible to everyone).

schema.title: Response List Types Api Leaves Types Get

post.description:
Create a leave type.

POST /api/leaves/types
Access: admin only. Side effects: writes a "leave_type.create" audit entry.

delete.description:
Delete (or soft-retire) a leave type.

DELETE /api/leaves/types/{type_id} -> 204 No Content
Access: admin only. Business rule: if any leave still references the type it is
deactivated (is_active=False) rather than deleted, to preserve referential
integrity; only unused types are hard-deleted.

get.description:
Return a tribe's leave configuration (approval requirement, overlap threshold).

GET /api/leaves/config?tribe_id=...
Access: any authenticated user, but only within their own leave-visibility
perimeter (can_see_leaves_of) - 403 otherwise.

put.description:
Update a tribe's leave configuration.

PUT /api/leaves/config?tribe_id=...
Access: admin or tribe leader; a tribe leader may only configure their own tribe.
Side effects: writes a "leave_config.update" audit entry.

get.description:
People the caller may file an absence for: themselves plus, for leaders,
everyone in their manageable scope. Used to populate the person picker.

List leaves matching the given filters, within the caller's tribe scope.

GET /api/leaves?from=&to=&user_id=&squad_id=&status=&mine=
Access: any authenticated user; non-admins are limited to their own tribe.
Filters: date overlap (from/to), a single user (or mine=true), a squad's
members, and status. Serialization hides comments the caller may not see.

schema.title: Response List Leaves Api Leaves Get

post.description:
File a leave for oneself or, for a manager, on behalf of someone in scope.

POST /api/leaves
Access: any authenticated user for their own leave; filing on behalf of another
requires manage rights over that person (can_manage_leave) - 403 otherwise.
Business rules: validates the type/detail/date range; whether approval is needed
comes from the target's tribe (leaves_require_approval), but a manager filing is
an approver so the leave is auto-approved.
Side effects: writes a "leave.create" audit entry.

put.description:
Edit a leave.

PUT /api/leaves/{leave_id}
Access: the owner (while pending or approved) or a manager of the person.
Business rules: re-validates type/detail/range; if the owner (not a manager)
edits an approved leave in a tribe that requires approval, it is reset to
pending and its decision fields cleared. A manager's edit keeps the status.

delete.description:
Delete a leave.

DELETE /api/leaves/{leave_id} -> 204 No Content
Access: the owner or a manager of the person - 403 otherwise.
Side effects: writes a "leave.delete" audit entry.

post.description:
Approve or reject a leave.

POST /api/leaves/{leave_id}/decision
Access: a manager of the person the leave belongs to (can_manage_leave) - 403
otherwise. Records the decider/decision time/comment.
Side effects: writes a "leave.decision" audit entry.

get.description:
Days where a squad has at least `overlap_threshold` people away at once,
restricted to the squads the caller can manage.

GET /api/leaves/overlaps?from=&to=
Access: leaders only (admin -> all squads, tribe leader -> own tribe, squad
leader -> led squads; others get an empty list); gated by `leaves >
overlap_alert`. The window is capped at ~1 year (422 beyond).

schema.title: Response Overlaps Api Leaves Overlaps Get

get.description:
Export leaves as a CSV attachment, within the caller's tribe scope.

GET /api/leaves/export.csv?from=&to=
Access: any authenticated user; non-admins limited to their own tribe. The
"Motif" column reflects the same comment-visibility rules as the API (only
populated for leaves whose comment the caller may see).

post.description:
Declare a committee for a squad.

POST /api/committees
Access: writer role + edit rights on the target squad (assert_can_edit_squad).
Side effects: records the creator and writes a "committee.create" audit entry.
Returns 404 if the referenced squad does not exist.

put.description:
Partially update a committee.

PUT /api/committees/{committee_id}
Access: writer role + edit rights on the committee's squad.
Side effects: writes a "committee.update" audit entry (with the changed fields).
Only fields present in the payload are applied (exclude_unset). 404 if unknown.

delete.description:
Delete a committee.

DELETE /api/committees/{committee_id} -> 204 No Content
Access: writer role + edit rights on the committee's squad.
Side effects: writes a "committee.delete" audit entry. 404 if unknown.

put.description: Turn Steerco reporting on/off for a squad (the squad leader's own toggle).

get.description:
Every steerco-enabled in-scope squad's snapshot for a period (drives the tab).

Leadership-only, like the one-pager endpoints it feeds: it returns every squad's
figures at once, which a squad member has no business reading.

One squad's monthly snapshot for a period ({} when not yet filled).

Also returns whether this month is already filled and when/who last updated it,
so the reporting screen can show the monthly-cadence status at a glance.

put.description: Create/update a squad's monthly snapshot for a period (writer + edit rights).

get.description: The 12 monthly snapshots ending at ``period`` (for the backfill grid).

put.description:
Backfill several months at once. Body: ``{"months": {"2026-06": {...}, ...}}``.

Each value is merged onto that month's snapshot (existing events are kept; the
numeric metrics that feed the charts are updated). Writer + edit rights.

get.description: One squad's KPI one-pager (auto-built from the last 12 monthly snapshots).

post.description:
Live preview of the one-pager for the wizard, using the still-unsaved snapshot
(``data``) as the current month. Nothing is persisted. Squad-leader accessible for
their own squad (they are the one filling the report).

get.description: Consolidated steerco (all steerco-enabled squads) as HTML one-pagers.
Consolidated steerco as PPTX. 501 when python-pptx is unavailable.
Liveness probe: cheap, unauthenticated, no DB access.
Public (pre-login) app configuration the SPA needs to render its shell.

AccessApproveIn.title: AccessApproveIn
AccessApproveIn.description: Admin decision when validating a pending account: final role and placement.

AuditOut.title: AuditOut
AuditOut.description: An audit-log entry as returned by the API.

AuthConfig.title: AuthConfig
AuthConfig.description: Which SSO backends are enabled, so the SPA can show the right login options.

AuthorInfo.title: AuthorInfo
AuthorInfo.description: Minimal identity of a feed post/reply author for display.

CommitteeCreate.title: CommitteeCreate
CommitteeCreate.description: Fields to declare a recurring governance meeting for a squad.

CommitteeOut.title: CommitteeOut
CommitteeOut.description: Committee as returned by the API.

DashboardOut.title: DashboardOut
DashboardOut.description: The full dashboard payload: the summary tiles plus one card per squad.

DashboardSummary.title: DashboardSummary
DashboardSummary.description: Aggregate headline figures across all squads in scope.

DependentItemOut.title: DependentItemOut
DependentItemOut.description: A milestone in another squad that depends on the squad being viewed.

FeedPostCreate.title: FeedPostCreate
FeedPostCreate.description: Body to publish a feed post (optionally scoped to a squad).

FeedPostOut.title: FeedPostOut
FeedPostOut.description: A feed post as returned, with replies, reaction tallies and the caller's own reactions.

FeedReplyCreate.title: FeedReplyCreate
FeedReplyCreate.description: Body to reply to a feed post.

FeedReplyOut.title: FeedReplyOut
FeedReplyOut.description: A feed reply as returned by the API.

InitiativeCreate.title: InitiativeCreate
InitiativeCreate.description: Fields to create a tribe-level strategic initiative.

InitiativeOut.title: InitiativeOut
InitiativeOut.description: Initiative as returned, with the assigned squad's name resolved for display.

KeyMessageCreate.title: KeyMessageCreate
KeyMessageCreate.description: Fields to add a curated executive message (year comes from the route/context).

KeyMessageOut.title: KeyMessageOut
KeyMessageOut.description: Key message as returned by the API.

KeyMessageUpdate.title: KeyMessageUpdate
KeyMessageUpdate.description: Partial edit of a key message.

KpiCreate.title: KpiCreate
KpiCreate.description: Fields to create a KPI on a squad.

KpiOut.title: KpiOut
KpiOut.description: KPI as returned by the API.

LeaderInfo.title: LeaderInfo
LeaderInfo.description: Minimal identity of a squad/tribe leader for display in cards and details.

LeaveConfigIn.title: LeaveConfigIn
LeaveConfigIn.description: Update of a tribe's leave policy (tribe leader / admin).

LeaveConfigOut.title: LeaveConfigOut
LeaveConfigOut.description: A tribe's current leave policy (approval requirement + overlap threshold).

LeaveDecisionIn.title: LeaveDecisionIn
LeaveDecisionIn.description: A leader's approve/reject decision on a pending leave request.

LeaveIn.title: LeaveIn
LeaveIn.description: Body to declare an absence over a date range (half-days on the edges).

LeaveOut.title: LeaveOut
LeaveOut.description:
A declared absence as returned, with resolved type/label, computed day
count and per-viewer permission flags (can_edit / can_decide). The private
``comment`` is null when the viewer is not allowed to see the motif.

LeaveOverlapDay.title: LeaveOverlapDay
LeaveOverlapDay.description: A day where a squad's simultaneous absences reach/exceed the warning threshold.

LeaveTypeIn.title: LeaveTypeIn
LeaveTypeIn.description: Create/update body for an absence category (admin-managed).

LeaveTypeOut.title: LeaveTypeOut
LeaveTypeOut.description: A configurable absence category as returned by the API.

LeaveUpdate.title: LeaveUpdate
LeaveUpdate.description: Partial edit of a declared absence.

LoginIn.title: LoginIn
LoginIn.description: Credentials submitted to the local (password) login endpoint.

MemberCreate.title: MemberCreate
MemberCreate.description: Fields to add a person to a squad's org chart (optionally linked to a login).

MemberOut.title: MemberOut
MemberOut.description: Squad member as returned by the API.

MemberUpdate.title: MemberUpdate
MemberUpdate.description: Partial edit of a squad member.

NotificationOut.title: NotificationOut
NotificationOut.description: A single in-app notification as returned by the API.

NotificationsResponse.title: NotificationsResponse
NotificationsResponse.description: A page of notifications plus the unread badge count.

ObjectiveCreate.title: ObjectiveCreate
ObjectiveCreate.description: Fields to create an annual squad objective.

ObjectiveOut.title: ObjectiveOut
ObjectiveOut.description: Objective as returned by the API (rag_status is server-derived).

OrgNodeCreate.title: OrgNodeCreate
OrgNodeCreate.description: Fields to create a node in a tribe's editable org chart.

OrgNodeTree.title: OrgNodeTree
OrgNodeTree.description:
A recursive org-chart node with its children nested underneath.

``squad_status`` is the derived health of the linked squad (when the node
maps to one), surfaced so the tree can colour-code squads.

OrgNodeUpdate.title: OrgNodeUpdate
OrgNodeUpdate.description: Partial edit of an org-chart node (incl. re-parenting via parent_id).

OtdCreate.title: OtdCreate
OtdCreate.description: Fields to create an OTD (top-management budget delivery commitment).

OtdMembers.title: OtdMembers
OtdMembers.description: Set of milestone ids that make up an OTD (tribe/admin manages membership).

PinIn.title: PinIn
PinIn.description: Body to pin or unpin a feed post.

QuarterProgressIn.title: QuarterProgressIn
QuarterProgressIn.description: Upsert a squad's completion percentage (0-100) for one year/quarter.

QuarterProgressOut.title: QuarterProgressOut
QuarterProgressOut.description: A squad's quarterly progress entry as returned.

ReactionIn.title: ReactionIn
ReactionIn.description: Body to add/toggle a reaction on a feed post.

ReportSubscriptionIn.title: ReportSubscriptionIn
ReportSubscriptionIn.description: Create/update a report subscription; empty weekdays unsubscribes.

ReportSubscriptionOut.title: ReportSubscriptionOut
ReportSubscriptionOut.description: A user's report email subscription and its schedule, as returned.

ReviewActionCreate.title: ReviewActionCreate
ReviewActionCreate.description: Fields to record a new review action item.

ReviewActionOut.title: ReviewActionOut
ReviewActionOut.description: A review/COPIL action item as returned by the API.

ReviewActionUpdate.title: ReviewActionUpdate
ReviewActionUpdate.description: Partial edit of a review action (incl. marking it done).

RoadmapItemCreate.title: RoadmapItemCreate
RoadmapItemCreate.description: Fields to create a roadmap milestone; theme and release_stage are mandatory here.

RoadmapItemOut.title: RoadmapItemOut
RoadmapItemOut.description: Roadmap milestone as returned; includes resolved dependency/OTD display labels.

RoadmapItemUpdate.title: RoadmapItemUpdate
RoadmapItemUpdate.description: Partial edit of a roadmap milestone (note: otd_id is managed from the OTD side).

SnapshotMeta.title: SnapshotMeta
SnapshotMeta.description: Snapshot header without the heavy payload, for listing past submissions.

SnapshotOut.title: SnapshotOut
SnapshotOut.description: A stored report snapshot including its frozen JSON payload.

SquadBudgetIn.title: SquadBudgetIn
SquadBudgetIn.description:
Budget figures submitted for a squad; who may set which field is role-gated
server-side (only tribe leaders may set the total envelope).

SquadBudgetOut.title: SquadBudgetOut
SquadBudgetOut.description: Budget as returned, with status/percentages/overrun derived server-side.

SquadCard.title: SquadCard
SquadCard.description: One squad's condensed status for the dashboard grid (progress + risk signals).

SquadCreate.title: SquadCreate
SquadCreate.description:
Fields to create a squad. budget_enabled is intentionally not settable here
(a tribe leader turns budget on afterwards via SquadUpdate).

SquadDetail.title: SquadDetail
SquadDetail.description:
Full squad report: SquadOut plus all nested content and computed roll-ups.

``budget`` is only populated for privileged viewers (squad leader, its tribe
leader, admins); other viewers receive it as None.

SquadOut.title: SquadOut
SquadOut.description: Squad summary as returned by the API (without the heavy nested report).

SquadUpdate.title: SquadUpdate
SquadUpdate.description: Partial edit of a squad (incl. toggling budget tracking and moving tribe).

SubmitCycleIn.title: SubmitCycleIn
SubmitCycleIn.description: Request to submit a reporting cycle, freezing the current report as a snapshot.

TribeCreate.title: TribeCreate
TribeCreate.description: Fields to create a tribe, optionally promoting a user to lead it.

TribeOrg.title: TribeOrg
TribeOrg.description: A tribe's full org chart as a forest of OrgNodeTree roots.

TribeOut.title: TribeOut
TribeOut.description: Tribe as returned by the API.

UserCreate.title: UserCreate
UserCreate.description: Fields to create a user account manually (password optional for SSO-only users).

UserOut.title: UserOut
UserOut.description: Public view of a user account returned by the API.

UserUpdate.title: UserUpdate
UserUpdate.description: Partial edit of a user account; only provided fields are changed.

ApiKeyAuth.description: Clé d'API créée dans Admin > API, envoyée en `Authorization: Bearer <clé>`.
